page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for several multi-threaded and compute-intensive tasks, including Geekbench multi-core performance, Passmark CPU score, and Passmark database operations. The processor's advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), enables strong (top 25%) performance in extended instruction tests and encryption. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput also reach top-tier levels. However, single-core stress-ng performance and Passmark memory scores remain in the average (middle 50%) category, highlighting a performance profile optimized for highly parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded execution.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-96 offers high compute density with its 96 dedicated vCPUs, making it efficient for parallel processing tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff, as memory-bound applications may experience bottlenecks, reflected in the average memory benchmark scores. Additionally, the complete absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the storage configuration.

This instance is configured for compute-heavy, parallelized workloads. It is suitable for multi-threaded software compilation, image processing, text processing, database operations, and static web serving. The presence of AMX and AVX-512 instructions also supports medium-scale LLM inference tasks, where prompt processing benchmarks demonstrate strong to top-tier execution speeds.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance equipped with 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz, and 192.0 GB of RAM. It features no local storage and no GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier (top 10%) performance in multi-core Geekbench, Passmark CPU, and database operations, alongside strong (top 25%) results in encryption and memory bandwidth. However, single-core stress-ng performance and memory scores are average (middle 50%). The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly efficient for compute-dense parallel workloads but less optimal for memory-heavy applications. This instance is suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for high-compute parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per vCPU allocation ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and LLM inference
